Ladies and gentlemen, this is going to be an incredible piece of knowledge for you to add to your arsenal as an entrepreneur, let alone a digital agency owner. But, as a digital agency owner, every single thing that everybody in our entire team does, including ourselves, can be statusized, looked at as a graph, and we can predict a condition based on what's going to happen to the graph next, and we can implement a series of actions based on the condition of a statistic in order to overcome it and get it to a more positive condition, in order to move into a fluence long term, comparatively going down, crashing and burning, putting you in a funky state as a digital agency owner. So, let's first understand statistics and why it's important to broaden your entire company off of stats. So, in any position I have, everybody runs and reports to me on statistics. So, by the digital marketer, he sends me a daily report, or is supposed to send me a daily report on everything that is going on, on every account for each of the clients that he's representing, making sure that he's sending me things on the amount spent each day, the clicks for that campaign that was spent on, and then the conversions that occurred from those efforts. Now, being said, if you don't get those reports from those people, you have them sign what's called a no report report, and long term that puts them in a bad position because it makes them less applicable for your bonuses, for your team efforts. If you have some kind of team bonus or incentive, if they have a certain amount and no report reports, that's where you're going to have them excluded from those team events and make them aware of those things. So, there has to be incentives, but there also has to be consequences, negative consequences to the game that you have people playing with. Anyway, as a digital marketer in a business, that's what you can hold them to statistically, and you can measure these things on a graph. So, if you want to step it up one level, you can have one position in your organization, or you can have one company, and I'll refer you to a few of you guys who like, click on the links below, it's one called envisage.io. That's going to give you guys an opportunity to have one dashboard of all your stats in it. And the moral of the story is that each position from a social media manager to the engagement, the impressions, the audience reach, whatever stats you want to have, the conversion, the link clicks, the messages sent, received, blah, blah, blah. Everything can be visualized, statusized, projects completed for a project manager, revenue generated for a salesperson, appointment set for a salesperson. Each thing can be statusized. So, check it out. When you have a graph, we'll just ask ourselves a question really quick, right? If you have a graph and you see that you have a salesperson on, say, makes a sale here and then, you know, he only makes this amount of sales on this day, and then he makes this many sales on this day, but then the next day he makes this many, then the next day he makes this many, then this many, then this many, then this many. What's the overall trend as we connect these data points here and see which direction the stat is going? You can see that it's going down. We can clearly see that our trend is pointing in this direction. But what do we do now that we know that it's trending down? Do we just say that it's trending down and do we bring it to the attention of the individual? Or is there a set list of actions that we can follow in order to overcome this, almost like a template or a formula, based on where graphs are at? Well, let me ask you this. If you saw your revenue, for example, go from this to this to this to this to this to this, and your graph just went like this, is this a little more critical and would you give a little more attention to this? And would you maybe take some different actions when something is going down this severity instead of this? If it has more severity to the actual action, are you going to take a different set of actions in order to overcome it? Or are you going to take the same actions for these two graphs? Comparatively, what's going to happen if you have something that's trending up but it's not going too fast? It's just going up. Or what happens if you have something that goes straight up like this? There's a different set of actions you take for the different results that you're seeking or the overall direction that you want your graphs and your stats to end up. If you look at things this way, it's almost like you game a fight for your entire life because you have a scoreboard for everything that you do. It's really interesting as well because it gives you an ability to not emotionally hold people accountable in a easier way to see if people are going to actually follow what you need them to do in order to accomplish these actions. So there's a set number and set amount of conditions is what they're called that we're going to go through together. I'm going to make sure that you guys have an understanding of these. That way you guys, when you guys are either looking at your Facebook ads analytics or you're using Sprout Social, which there's a link for below for social media analytic reporting. Or there's any other tool that you choose to look at that has a graph. I want you to be educated on what the graph condition is and what you need to do next in order to get it to the next state. So let's first understand this. Non-existence, danger, emergency, normal, affluence, and power are the different conditions that we are going to go over together for stats within your business. Non-existence, if I drew it on a graph, would look like this. It doesn't exist. What do you do when you just get a job and you walk in somewhere and you're looking to do work? Well, you have to create a communication line. That's actually step number one when something's in non-existence and you want to move it to the danger condition. You see, each thing here moves to the next step. So non-existence moves into danger. Danger moves into emergency. Emergency moves into normal. Normal moves into affluence. Affluence moves into power. So if something's in non-existence state, the next logical step for it is to trend down. So what does that mean? It just means that if you would have a cumulative amount of data after more than just one day of getting something out of non-existence, its likelihood of going back into non-existence is one step away. Does that make sense? Yes, because if you're one step away from going from danger to emergency or from emergency to normal, there's also one step from going away to going back down. So the same thing occurs. You could be wherever you're at. You could be at normal going back down to emergency. You could be in danger and you could go back down to non-existence. This is a trail up and it's a trail right back down. Does that make sense? Good. Check this out. Emergency looks more like this. It's more like this or it can also be anything that is normally like flat because nothing stays the same. Things are either getting better or they're getting worse. So even though this graph might be flat or your accumulated data might overall trend flat, that means that it's in an emergency condition. Don't misconstrue. When you have an emergency though, we'll just make this line a little more downward right here. It looks like this. It's going down, but it's not like horrid like the danger condition would be. And I'm just giving you a visual right now on what each one of these graphs would look like for your accumulated data. Then we'll go over each one in more detail about what it means, why it means that, and what it takes to get it to the next step or what's going to happen to get it to go down. Now your normal condition is just slightly trending up. So it looks more like this. Now affluence, affluence is pretty tricky because affluence is just, wha! Affluence just means you're going up to a rocket ship. Affluence is a very tricky condition because what you're trying to do next is what's called power. Now power is the greatest condition that you can go into. So let's say you're normally trending right here, right? This is your normal trend. So you're going up. All of a sudden affluence happens. Now affluence happens, keep in mind if you're in a normal state. So things are going well, and then all of a sudden, wha! You go into affluence. Most people in life, after they go into affluence, they don't know what to do. They don't know the condition steps. They don't know how to handle it. More importantly, they slip into euphoria. They completely fuck themselves up. They go right back down. That sucks. Because they fall from affluence, back down, they stabilize into a normal state. Some people go from affluence right past normal and they go right into an emergency condition. Other people go into a danger condition and they stay in a danger condition depending on the momentum that they have downward. We'll talk more about that in a second. So, power is where you trend normally again that at a higher rate than you were trending at normal prior to going into affluence. So power is where you've embraced the affluence, you've successfully applied the condition formula, and you've moved into the next phase of power, which is trending at a new rate than you were before at that normal level. Now let's go into detail here. You might have a graph that's just going down like your finances or your ability to deliver for clients or your clients, just like my graph was during that month. When anything in your own business is in this condition, focus more attention on it unless you want it to die because it's one step away from non-existence. So here, I'm technically in an emergency because nothing's getting better and nothing's getting worse. I technically lost revenue the next two months and I should have learned the lessons of my current state. I lost my product limit right here but I didn't. So I lost a little bit of business but then I popped into affluence again because technically I was still in a normal trend here. You just skipped right over normal and you're in affluence. Keep in mind this is accumulated data. So your normal trend can look just like this. It can go up, it can go down, it can go up, it can go down, it can go up. But the overall trend is going up. You see that? So it's the same thing here. I was able to pop into affluence because it's a normal trend. Now check this out. You see these months, you didn't know where I went down and it felt like a world was crashing down on me. Because this is still a normal trend. It really wasn't a danger condition. However, day by day, your conditions matter.
